title: Peakstone Result Bundle
description: A signed, self-contained record of one model configuration ('run') scored on a suite. Embeds everything needed to independently reproduce deterministic results: exact model identity (repo + revision + file hash + quant + engine + sampling + serve flags), environment, content-hashed challenges, full transcripts, and scores. This file is the contract between the engine and the API; it is content-addressed (bundle_hash = sha256 of the canonical JSON with `submitter.signature` omitted) and the submitter signs that hash.

submitter.description: Optional for local/anonymous runs; required to claim a submission on the platform.

signature.description: ed25519 signature over bundle_hash (base64). Excluded from the hash itself.

version.description: Suite version, e.g. '2026.06'. Leaderboards are scoped per (suite, version).

content_hash.description: sha256 over the concatenated challenge content-hashes in the suite — pins the exact challenge set scored.

aggregate.description: Convenience rollups; authoritative scores are recomputed server-side from `results`.

bundle_hash.description: sha256 of the canonical JSON (signature omitted). Self-reported; the server recomputes and verifies it.

model.description: Identity of the EXACT weights + how they were run — the reproducibility core. (artifact + quant + serve config define a distinct 'run'; runs are never collapsed.)

family.description: Base model family, for grouping in evolution charts (e.g. 'Qwen3-Coder-Next').

hf_revision.description: Git revision/commit of the HF repo — pins the exact weights version.

file_sha256.description: SHA-256 of the model file(s). For split GGUFs, hash of each part.

file_sha256_verified.description: False if file_sha256 is a placeholder (skipped/missing/incomplete) rather than a real hash — consumers must not treat it as a reproducibility anchor.

release_date.description: Model release date — the x-axis of the evolution chart, and the CONSERVATIVE, unforgeable contamination boundary: a model cannot have trained on a challenge published after it was released. The official held-out score keys off this.

training_cutoff.description: Claimed knowledge/training-data cutoff. Tighter (more generous to the model) than release_date but self-reported, so it backs only a secondary 'claimed-clean' view, never the official metric. Optional — omit when unknown.

seed.description: Required to be reproducible when temperature > 0.

serve_flags.description: Full serve command flags (offload, KV type, etc.) — part of the run identity.

result.description: One challenge's outcome. additionalProperties allowed for capability-specific fields (JSONB-style extensibility).

challenge_hash.description: sha256 of the challenge's content (spec + tests + meta) — pins the exact challenge version scored.

published_at.description: When this challenge's content first became public. Compared against the model's release_date to decide contamination: clean iff published_at > release_date. Authoritative value is the platform's first-seen timestamp per challenge_hash (unforgeable); this field carries the engine's best local estimate.

published_at_source.description: Provenance of published_at, so consumers can weight its trust: 'upstream' = the originating benchmark/contest date, 'platform-first-seen' = the platform notarized this content_hash, 'git' = first commit in the corpus, 'author' = self-declared, 'unknown'.

capabilities.description: Coarse capability axis required by the challenge.

difficulty.description: Author SEED difficulty; the platform calibrates the real tier (T0-T4) from submission data.

attempts.description: Number of generations (>1 = a --retries self-repair loop ran).

metrics.description: No-LLM efficiency axes (deterministic sub-scores, separate from the correctness score): loc, solution_bytes, peak_rss_mb, test_wall_s, ...

env.description: goal-state-env provenance: provider, image digests, verifier checks, turns-to-green.

transcript.description: Inline for small suites; for large ones, a content-addressed reference (transcript_ref) may replace this.

plan.description: For planner runs: the spec/plan the coder executed.

transcript_ref.description: Content-addressed blob id, if the transcript is stored out-of-band.

judge.description: Present only for verification=llm-judge; makes the score 'soft' (not auto-verifiable).

verification_method.description: How the result was scored — drives the trust tier. deterministic-tests are independently re-runnable; llm-judge/goal-state/human are 'soft'.
